title: Agent Safety Checklist
concept: Checking: input filtered, action pre-flights active, output scanned, cost capped, safety model registered.

exercises.prompt: What can happen if we don’t check an agent’s actions?

options.text: The agent could make mistakes, like sending unauthorized emails.

exercises.explain: It's crucial to check an agent's actions to prevent mistakes, like sending unauthorized emails.

cards.title: Agent Safety Checklist
cards.body: To keep agents safe, we use a checklist. This checklist helps us make sure they are ready to work properly and safely.

cards.title: What is an Agent Safety Checklist?
cards.body: An Agent Safety Checklist is a list of checks to ensure that the agent will operate safely before it starts doing its tasks.

cards.title: Building a Safe Machine
cards.body: Think of an agent as a machine you are building. Before starting it, you need to check all parts. If one part is broken, the machine might not work right.

cards.title: Using the Checklist
cards.body: Before an agent works, we check: Is input filtered? Are actions ready? Is output scanned? Is the cost controlled? Is the safety model in place?

cards.title: Myth: Checklists Are Unnecessary
cards.body: Some think safety checklists waste time. In reality, they prevent mistakes and ensure agents run properly!

cards.title: Checking Each Step
cards.body: Every item on the checklist is important for safety.

left.text: Is the input checked to prevent bad data?

right.text: Are actions checked for safety before they happen?

exercises.prompt: Which item is NOT part of the agent safety checklist?
exercises.explain: Ignoring the cost means not checking if the agent's actions are too expensive, which is not safe.

exercises.prompt: True or False: All parts of the agent safety checklist are optional.
exercises.explain: False! All parts of the checklist are necessary to ensure the agent operates safely.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Each checklist helps ensure the agent's __________ is controlled.
exercises.sentence: Each checklist helps ensure the agent's cost is controlled.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'cost', which refers to managing expenses related to the agent's actions.

exercises.prompt: Which option describes a correct part of the agent safety checklist?

options.text: Making decisions by itself all the time.
Filtering input to avoid harmful data.
Waiting for no checks before starting.

exercises.explain: Filtering input helps protect the agent from using dangerous or harmful data.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is included in the agent safety checklist?

options.text: Input filtering to block bad data.

exercises.explain: Input filtering is crucial for safety, as it ensures the agent only receives valid and safe data to work with.
